Patch Set 1:

> Its a nice start but linux/uuid.h your using is an UAPI header. For
 > kernel sources it should be #include <uapi/linux/uuid.h>

There is also a kernel <linux/uuid.h> header that includes the UAPI header (and defines uuid_t, but that isn't in userspace), so it works properly for both userspace and in the kernel.

This allows getting rid of a bunch of kernel compatibility cruft (-100 LOC), so I'm surprised you are against it.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

I was wrong. My thinking was guid was all in the uapi header but some of the guid handling is only in the Linux kernel header. The original reason for the libcfs header was RHEL7 lacked uuid support. Guess RHEL7.9 supports it now.

Patch Set 1:

Zam, can you please also review this, as the next patch to add wirecheck on the scrub_file structs is depending on it.
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as 20621c761b787cd7b08a9d0abc0fc362f0bb0122 by Oleg Drokin

parents.subject: LU-16163 tests: skip racer_on_nfs for NFSv3

commit.subject: LU-16655 scrub: change sf_uuid to guid_t
commit.message:
LU-16655 scrub: change sf_uuid to guid_t

Change the type of sf_uuid from uuid_t to guid_t.  The sizes are
identical, but the benefit is that guid_t is usable in userspace
with the <linux/uuid.h> header, unlike uuid_t.

Change the accessors to use the corresponding guid_*() functions,
but no functional changes are needed.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I3048cfee20a5e4ea2c0c2203d22eb76c1437577b
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/50496
